FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Namespace FluentGpu.Signals

Classes

Computation

A reactive computation — the base for effects, memos and component render-effects. Holds the set of sources it read last run (so it can unlink before re-tracking) and an owner tree of nested computations + cleanups (disposed on re-run / dispose). Subclasses define what running means.

Effect

A reactive side-effect: runs body once now, then re-runs whenever a signal it READ changes (deferred to the next Flush()). A property binding (signal → scene node) is an Effect; a component render is a specialized effect. Dispose to stop it (also runs registered cleanups).

FloatSignal

A specialized scalar signal for the hot high-frequency path (slider scrub, scroll offset, progress) — avoids the generic boxing/comparer indirection so a per-pointer-move write is allocation-free. Bind it to a node channel (transform/paint) for a value→pixels update that skips render/reconcile/layout entirely.

ManagedEffect

A subclassable reactive computation for callers that own the run body (e.g. a component render-effect that re-runs Render() + reconciles its subtree). The host calls Schedule behaviour via the runtime; subclasses implement Execute().

Memo<T>

A derived reactive value (the Solid createMemo / Vue computed): recomputes lazily from the signals it reads, caches the result, and only notifies its own subscribers when the cached value actually changes. It is both a computation (it subscribes upstream) and a source (downstream subscribes to it).

Prop

A unified reactive channel property: ONE field that is EITHER a static value (re-asserted by the reconciler on every reconcile of the element, iff not bound) OR a reactive bind — a Func<TResult> thunk or a concrete signal the reconciler wires into a mount-time effect that writes exactly one scene column and marks its dirty axis. Constructed implicitly: Opacity = 0.5f (static), Opacity = () => f(sig.Value) (derived thunk), Opacity = sig (signal-direct — no user closure). default(Prop<T>) is the static default(T) — never use it to mean "unset"; element property initializers (= 1f, = NaN) run the T conversion and survive with clones (a bound clone stays bound). Kind discrimination is a runtime type test on the single payload ref — no tag byte; the static reconcile path reads only IsBound (one null test). Conversions from the IReadSignal<T> INTERFACE are illegal in C# (CS0552) — through an interface-typed variable use the thunk form chan = () => s.Value.

Reactive

The reactive core: a Solid/Preact-style fine-grained reactivity graph. Signals are observable cells; computations (effects, memos, component render-effects) auto-subscribe to the signals they READ during a run, and re-run when a read signal changes. This is the single update mechanism the whole engine is built on — a property binding is an effect at node granularity; a component re-render is an effect at subtree granularity.

Threading: UI-thread-confined (the engine's single render/reconcile thread), so tracking state is ThreadStaticAttribute. Scheduling is DEFERRED: a signal write marks dependent effects stale and asks the host for a frame; the host drains them once per frame via Flush() (phase 3), which keeps reconcile/layout on their phases and the per-frame paint allocation-free.

ReactiveRuntime

The scheduler: owns the pending-effect queue, the batch depth, and the "a frame is needed" callback the host wires to wake its loop. One per AppHost (so headless tests don't cross-contaminate). Single-threaded.

ReactiveScope

A stable owner that never re-runs — a container for child computations + cleanups. Used as a component's lifetime scope (its render-effect + bindings live under it) and as the app root. Disposing it cascades to everything it owns.

Signal<T>

A reactive cell: reading Value inside a computation subscribes it; writing notifies subscribers (deferred — they re-run on the next Flush()). The unit of state in the engine; a UseState is a Signal whose subscriber is the owning component's render-effect.

Structs

Prop<T>

Interfaces

IReadSignal<T>

Read-only view of a reactive value (a Signal<T> or a Memo<T>).

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
